answer: Higgsfield authentication depends on your method of access. For the official v2 SDK, the recommended approach is using a single credentials string in the format KEY_ID:KEY_SECRET, passed via the config function or the HF_CREDENTIALS environment variable [1][2]. When calling the API directly via HTTP, use the Authorization: Key KEY_ID:KEY_SECRET header [1][2] or a Bearer token [3]. For CLI users, run higgsfield auth login to handle authentication automatically [4][5]. Unofficial integrations may alternatively support Clerk-based session tokens or environment-based API key and secret pairs [6][7][8].
